key_points: India is located in South Asia and occupies a central position in the continent of Asia
The Indian mainland extends between latitudes 8°4'N and 37°6'N
The Tropic of Cancer passes almost through the middle of India
It divides India into tropical and subtropical climatic regions
India covers a geographical area of about 3.28 million square kilometres
India ranks seventh among the largest countries of the world
The Indian land boundary is about 15,200 kilometres long
India's total coastline including islands is about 7,516.6 kilometres
The Standard Meridian of India is 82°30'E longitude
Indian Standard Time is based on the Standard Meridian passing near Mirzapur
India's central location helped in establishing trade relations with other countries
Ancient trade routes connected India with Europe, Africa and Southeast Asia
The Indian Ocean helped India maintain maritime contacts with other regions
India exported goods like spices, muslin and silk in ancient times
examples: Example of trade contact: Indian spices traded with European countries
Example of cultural contact: Spread of Indian numerals to other regions
Example of island group: Lakshadweep Islands
Example of island group: Andaman and Nicobar Islands
formulas: Total area of India ≈ 3.28 million sq km
exam_tips: Understand the importance of India's central location in Asia
Remember the geographical coordinates of India
Study the neighbouring countries of India carefully
Learn the importance of the Indian Ocean in trade
Practice map-based questions related to seas, neighbours and island groups
